commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But Saul had given Michal his daughter, David's wife, to Palti the son of Laish, who was of Gallim. Phalti, or Phaltiel, 2 Kings iii. Saul violated all laws by so doing, and David took her back when he came to the throne, which he could not have done if he had given her a bill of divorce, Deuteronomy xxiv. (Calmet) Michol was not blameless in living thus with another man. (Menochius) The Rabbins say that a sword hindered Phalti from approaching her. (Horn in Sulp. ) Gallim, a city of Benjamin, Isaias x.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
